description: A dual-axis line graph presenting the calculated Vickers hardness ($H_V$, red squares, left y-axis in GPa) and Debye temperature ($\Theta_D$, blue squares, right y-axis in K) as functions of Mn content ($x$) in the Fe$_{1-x}$Mn$_x$Al alloy system, spanning from FeAl ($x$=0) to MnAl ($x$=1.0). Both properties exhibit a similar non-monotonic trend: they decrease sharply from FeAl (approximately 16.5 GPa and 652.5 K) to a minimum at Fe$_{0.5}$Mn$_{0.5}$Al ($x$=0.5, approximately 6.7 GPa and 533.3 K), subsequently increase to a local maximum near Fe$_{0.25}$Mn$_{0.75}$Al ($x$=0.75, approximately 9.8 GPa and 574.2 K), and finally decrease toward MnAl (approximately 5.0 GPa and 504.1 K). This correlation suggests that Mn addition weakens covalent bonding character up to the equiatomic composition, with the synchronous variation of hardness and Debye temperature reflecting the underlying changes in chemical bonding and elastic properties across the alloy series.
